description.en: Return a readable and writable view over the contents of the buffer without copying them. Also, mutating the view will transparently update the contents of the buffer: >>> b = io.BytesIO(b"abcdef") >>> view = b.getbuffer() >>> view[2:4] = b"56" >>> b.getvalue() b'ab56ef' Note As long as the view exists, the BytesIO object cannot be resized or closed. Added in version 3.2.

excerpt.es: Return a readable and writable view over the contents of the buffer without copying them. Also, mutating the view will transparently update the contents of the buffer: >>> b = io.BytesIO(b"abcdef") >>...
excerpt.en: Return a readable and writable view over the contents of the buffer without copying them. Also, mutating the view will transparently update the contents of the buffer: >>> b = io.BytesIO(b"abcdef") >>...

description.en: Return bytes containing the entire contents of the buffer.

excerpt.es: Return bytes containing the entire contents of the buffer.
excerpt.en: Return bytes containing the entire contents of the buffer.

description.en: Return a str containing the entire contents of the buffer. Newlines are decoded as if by read(), although the stream position is not changed.

excerpt.es: Return a str containing the entire contents of the buffer. Newlines are decoded as if by read(), although the stream position is not changed.
excerpt.en: Return a str containing the entire contents of the buffer. Newlines are decoded as if by read(), although the stream position is not changed.
